item.title: Your First API Call | DeepSeek API Docs
item.description: The DeepSeek API uses an API format compatible with OpenAI/Anthropic. By modifying the configuration, you can use the OpenAI/Anthropic SDK or softwares compatible with the OpenAI/Anthropic API to access the DeepSeek API.

item.title: The Agent Observability Gap
item.description: Why agents that look healthy in a playground go sideways the moment they meet real users, and what a useful observability stack for agent-shaped systems has to cover.
item.author: (https://lyuata.com)

item.title: Canton vs Public Crypto: A Technical Comparison
item.description: Canton differs significantly from Ethereum and other L2s, focusing on local agreement and privacy. Transactions involve only relevant parties, enhancing scalability and confidentiality. Its declara…

item.title: Cooked
item.description: By all means, Tim Cook was a better choice for Apple CEO than most people you could pick out of a hat; perhaps even of high executives at Apple working at the time. But this judgement is not unalloyed.

item.title: Dharmateja Samudrala
item.description: Software Engineer — building things on the internet.

item.title: Dear Codebase: A Letter From Your Coding Agent
item.description: Ten requests from an agent to the codebase it works in — local reasoning, visible boundaries, one way of doing things. None of them are new. All of them matter more now.
item.author: (Przemek Mroczek)

item.title: Stop Hardcoding Your Timeouts
item.description: Hardcoded timeouts with no config options are a silent tax on developers outside the wealthy west. A rant about npx skills, Docker Gordon, and the arrogance of assuming everyone has a fast connection.
item.author: (Ahmad Elalfy)

item.title: The end of responsive images
item.description: Mat Marquis has waited 14 years to write this article. The sizes attribute has been a necessary evil but now, with an auto value capability, it’s completely transformed authoring responsive images on the web.

item.title: Cutting Costs, Not Corners: How We Migrated Our AI Document Pipeline from AWS to Cloudfleet
item.description: Introduction aka TL;DR; In January, we handled almost twice the workload we handled in December at a fifth of the cost. Background In th...

item.title: On commenting and approving pull requests
item.description: After reviewing a lot of pull requests, I’ve settled on a simple default: if my comments are all nitpicks, suggestions, questions, or non-blocking issues, I leave them and approve the PR at the same time.

item.title: Trajectory shapes
item.description: Benchmark scores tell you whether a coding agent solved the task. Trajectory shapes tell you how it worked, and those workflow signatures persist b...

item.title: Why I spent years trying to make CSS states predictable — tenphi.me
item.description: Why predictable CSS state resolution matters, and how Tasty approaches it with state maps and non-overlapping selectors.
item.author: (https://tenphi.me)

item.title: Approximating Hyperbolic Tangent
item.description: Survey of fast tanh approximations using Taylor series, Padé approximants, splines, and bitwise manipulation techniques like K-TanH and Schraudolph

item.title: Death to files and folders
item.description: Ode to Jef Raskin's quote, The content of a file is its own best name

item.title: Agency Is Not Autonomy | Mark Lubin
item.description: Two axes the AI agent field treats as one, and the design rule that follows from splitting them.

item.title: Going Full Time on Open Source
item.description: Jeff Dickey's personal website

item.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus | David Álvarez Rosa | Personal Website
item.description: Although the notion of area is intuitive, its mathematical treatment requires a rigorous definition. This post introduces the Riemann integral, and proves the …

item.title: Old computers in my university | Mr.UNIX
item.description: In my previous university, there was a hallway in the department of mathematics and computer science, where they were showcasing some of the old computers that we had.

item.title: Context Engineering and the Limits of Agentic Coding | Stephen Fritz
item.description: My work is context engineering. I didn't know the term until after I'd built a system that turned out to be exactly that.

item.title: Making beautiful work is getting harder
item.description: In the early days of LLMs, we had tools that helped us stay focused and thereby do better work. Then, in late 2024, everything began to change.

item.title: The OpenClaw turkey problem
item.description: Thoughts are mine and mine only. I don't ever use LLMs for writing.

item.title: Trust Me, I’m a Shortcut
item.description: Windows’ primary mechanism for shortcuts, LNK files, is frequently abused by threat actors for payload delivery and persistence. This blog post introduces several new LNK file flaws that, amongst other things, allow attackers to fully spoof an LNK’s target. It also introduces lnk-it-up, a tool suite that can generate such deceptive LNK files, as well as detect anomalous ones.

item.title: The Log is the Database
item.description:
foldb has one source of truth: an append-only, Raft-replicated log. Everything else, the storage layer, the indexes, the materialized query results, is just a fold(log). A pure function applied to a sequence of committed entries in order.
A transaction commits when its entry is durable in the log on

item.title: An update on recent Claude Code quality reports
item.description: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

item.title: Introducing GPT-5.5
item.description: Introducing GPT-5.5, our smartest model yet—faster, more capable, and built for complex tasks like coding, research, and data analysis across tools.

item.title: French govt agency confirms breach as hacker offers to sell data
item.description: France Titres, the government agency in France for issuing and managing administrative documents has disclosed a data breach after a threat actor claimed the attack and stealing citizen data.

item.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain ...
item.description: Bitwarden CLI 2026.4.0 was compromised in the Checkmarx supply chain campaign after attackers abused a GitHub Action in Bitwarden’s CI/CD pipeline.

item.title: crawshaw - 2026-04-22
Highlights from Git 2.54
item.description: The open source Git project just released Git 2.54. Here is GitHub’s look at some of the most interesting features and changes introduced since last time.
